description: Replace spdlog mini-implementation with a production-quality logging library that has async logging with lock-free ring buffer, multiple sink support (console with colors, rotating file), configurable format patterns, flush policies, and the same API surface as real spdlog.
steps: Rewrite third_party/spdlog/include/spdlog/spdlog.h with a full implementation (~800-1200 lines) keeping the same namespace and class names
Implement a lock-free ring buffer (power-of-2 size, atomic head/tail) for async message passing from producer threads to the logging backend thread
Implement a sink interface (abstract base class) with: log(level, formatted_message), flush(), set_level(level), should_log(level)

Implement stdout_color_sink_mt in spdlog/sinks/stdout_color_sinks.h: writes to stdout/stderr with ANSI color codes based on log level (trace=white, debug=cyan, info=green, warn=yellow, error=red, critical=bold red)
Implement basic_file_sink_mt in spdlog/sinks/basic_file_sink.h: writes to a file with optional truncation on open
Add a rotating_file_sink_mt: rotates files when they exceed max_size bytes, keeps up to max_files rotated copies (renames file.log to file.1.log, file.1.log to file.2.log, etc.)
Implement the spdlog::logger class with: multiple sinks vector, per-logger level, format pattern string, name, log() method that formats message then dispatches to all sinks

Implement format pattern parsing: support %Y (year), %m (month), %d (day), %H (hour), %M (minute), %S (second), %e (milliseconds), %n (logger name), %l (level), %t (thread id), %v (message), %s (source file), %# (line number)
Implement async logging mode: a background thread reads from ring buffer and writes to sinks. flush() blocks until ring buffer is drained
Implement flush policies: flush_on(level) flushes when a message at or above the given level is logged; periodic flush via flush_every(seconds)
Keep the existing free functions: spdlog::info(), spdlog::debug(), etc. that delegate to default_logger()

Ensure the existing Logger.cpp in src/core/src/ compiles unchanged - it uses spdlog::logger, spdlog::get(), spdlog::level, std::make_shared<spdlog::logger>(name), logger->set_level(), logger->trace/debug/info/warn/error/critical(fmt, args...)
Ensure all format string substitution uses the existing {} placeholder syntax with variadic template expansion
acceptance_criteria: Logger outputs colored messages to console (ANSI escape codes present)
File sink creates and writes to log files
Rotating file sink rotates when file exceeds configured size
Async logging mode does not block the calling thread
Thread-safe: multiple threads logging concurrently produces no data races
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/ThreatOne && cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF && cmake --build build 2>&1 | tail -5
